hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5 interactions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity with SSTR2 ECL2/ECL3, thereby increasing selectivity margin over off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: To achieve SSTR2 selectivity, we must prioritize mutations at non-pharmacophore positions that disrupt conserved off-target interactions. The next iteration should focus on these positions to increase Δmargin and ensure selectivity over SSTR1/3/4/5.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target binding (particularly to SSTR3/SSTR5). Focusing mutations on these positions can disrupt off-target interactions while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions in off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets while avoiding disruption of the pharmacophore (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
